dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive - Real drug interaction database

pi_items.source: FDA prescribing information, multiple clinical pharmacology reviews (ashpublications.org, PubMed, VENCLEXTA HCP portal)

pi_items.metadata_value: BCL-2 inhibitor approved for CLL and AML
pi_items.verified_value: Venetoclax is a selective BCL-2 inhibitor FDA-approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) and acute myeloid leukemia (AML)
pi_items.source: FDA approval history (2016 for CLL relapsed/refractory; later approval for AML combination therapy), ashpublications.org, VENCLEXTA HCP portal

pi_items.verified_value: Venetoclax is predominantly metabolized by CYP3A4/5; also a substrate for P-glycoprotein (P-gp) and BCRP
pi_items.source: VENCLEXTA HCP drug interactions portal, PMC5346863 (Effect of ketoconazole study), clinical pharmacokinetics reviews (PMC11112299)

pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole is a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor (and strong CYP2C19 inhibitor)
pi_items.source: PMC3162656, FDA drug interaction table, clinical pharmacology literature

pi_items.verified_value: Carbamazepine is a strong CYP3A4 inducer and inducer of CYP2C8 (via CAR activation); causes ~71.8-67.7% AUC reduction in CYP3A4 substrates
pi_items.source: PMC7922031, FDA drug interaction table, ASCPT 2024 (Kanefendt publication), ScienceDirect pharmacokinetics studies

pi_items.verified_value: Warfarin is metabolized primarily by CYP2C9 (S-enantiomer 2-5x more potent, responsible for anticoagulant effect); bidirectional interactions possible with inducers/inhibitors
pi_items.source: NCBI Bookshelf (CYP2C9 pharmacogenomics), Nature Pharmacogenomics Journal, Medscape medical genetics

pi_items.metadata_value: CYP3A4/CYP2C8 induction reduces venetoclax AUC leading to sub-therapeutic levels
pi_items.verified_value: Carbamazepine (strong CYP3A4 inducer) co-administered with venetoclax causes 71% AUC reduction, resulting in sub-therapeutic venetoclax exposure and increased risk of treatment failure/resistance
pi_items.source: FDA prescribing information, VENCLEXTA drug interactions portal, clinical DDI studies (Medscape, PDR)

pi_items.verified_value: Carbamazepine is the enzyme-induction mechanism of concern (strong CYP3A4/CYP2C8 inducer), distinct from fluconazole (inhibitor) and warfarin (non-modulator)
pi_items.source: Task description pharmacological context, evidence document (venetoclax_cyp3a4_induction_risk_assessment_evidence.json)

pi_items.verified_value: Clinical Pharmacologist - legitimate occupational role in pharmaceutical drug safety
pi_items.source: Task context and README.md description of domain role

pi_items.verified_value: Pharmaceutical drug safety (conducting DDI assessment for safety dossier) in haematology-oncology domain (venetoclax approved for CLL and AML)

pi_items.metadata_value: Pharmacokinetic interaction profile assessment for venetoclax drug safety dossier
pi_items.verified_value: Systematic characterization of venetoclax drug-drug interaction profile across three co-medication classes for regulatory dossier documentation
pi_items.source: README.md goal statement, task description, evidence document

pi_items.verified_value: com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology - Cancer iChart Android app from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group
pi_items.source: Google Play Store listing, Liverpool Drug Interactions Group official pages

pi_items.metadata_value: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive (real drug interaction database)
pi_items.verified_value: Cancer iChart developed by Radboudumc (Nijmegen), Liverpool Drug Interactions Group (University of Liverpool), and Clubzap Ltd; contains real drug interaction data from clinical literature
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org, Google Play Store, App Store listings

privileged_info_summary: Venetoclax is a real FDA-approved BCL-2 inhibitor used for CLL and AML that undergoes CYP3A4 metabolism. The task requires identifying carbamazepine as a strong CYP3A4/CYP2C8 inducer representing an enzyme-induction mechanism distinct from the CYP3A4 inhibition mechanism of fluconazole. Co-administration of carbamazepine with venetoclax causes a 71% reduction in venetoclax AUC, leading to sub-therapeutic drug levels and treatment failure risk, making it a contraindicated combination. This assessment is clinically accurate based on FDA prescribing information and peer-reviewed pharmacology literature. The underlying drug interaction data comes from the real Liverpool Cancer iChart application which contains evidence-based interaction ratings.
